content:
2 Spool Hydraulic Manifold

Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG28H

![Manual Control Valve](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/2-spool-hydraulic-manifold-01.webp)

The QFZMG28H load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed based on the LUDV principle and is suitable for closed-center variable displacement pump systems. Proportional flow distribution among multiple actuators is achieved through load pressure compensation. Targeted at high-pressure, high-flow applications, the valve is mainly used in construction machinery such as rotary drilling rigs and crawler cranes, where continuity of combined motions is critical.

 The integrated valve manifolds are custom-engineered products developed for the hydraulic systems of coal mine horizontal directional drilling rigs. These manifolds are specifically designed and integrated based on the individual requirements of the customer’s equipment. Through system-level integration, the manifold consolidates multiple control and auxiliary functions required by directional drilling rigs into a single valve manifold. This approach reduces external piping and dispersed valve components.

Features & Advantages

- Customized design: Functional configurations can be customized according to actual operating conditions, meeting the requirements for control accuracy and reliability during drilling, positioning, and auxiliary operations.

- Dedicated operating-condition adaptation: Supports integration of drilling-rig-specific functions such as drill rod loading and unloading, reverse floating control, and low-speed rotational positioning.

title: Hydraulic Directional Control Valve
content:
Hydraulic Directional Control Valve

5DP20GL Directional Control Valve

![Manual Control Valve](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/multi-spool-directional-control-valve-1-1-01m.webp)

![Hydraulic Control Valve](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/multi-spool-directional-control-valve-1-1-02m.webp)

The 5DP20GL multi spool directional-control valve is designed for the upper-structure control systems of 8-16-ton full-hydraulic truck cranes. It adopts a sectional-type directional-control structure with cast-in internal flow passages. By combining multiple valve sections, the valve enables centralized control of actuators including hoisting, luffing, telescoping, slewing, and winch functions.
 The valve body incorporates a series oil circuit design. Through spool shifting, hydraulic oil is distributed in an orderly sequence among individual circuits, meeting the requirements for both independent and combined crane motions.

Technical Specifications

| Fluid Media | Hydraulic oil |
|---|---|
| Mounting Type | Threaded connection |
| Control Type | Manual / Hydraulic |
| Seal Material | NBR / FKM (Viton) |
| Body Material | Cast iron |
| Nominal Diameter | 20mm |
| Max. Flow Rate | 250L/min |
| Max. Working Pressure | 315bar |

![Diagram](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/multi-spool-directional-control-valve-1-1-03m.webp)
 Diagram

![Dimension Drawing](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/multi-spool-directional-control-valve-1-1-04m.webp)
 Dimension Drawing

Features & Advantages

- Winch operation adaptation: Supports dual-pump combination with a five-position spool and double-mode control, suitable for winch operation under varying load conditions.

- Compact structure: The cast-in internal flow passage sectional structure reduces external piping, facilitating compact layout and system integration.

- Series oil circuit design: Clear oil supply logic with predictable motion response.

- Customizable matching: Circuit layouts and configurations can be adjusted according to overall machine operating conditions.

The DL series hydraulic directional-control valves are sectional, modular hydraulic control valves designed for applications in mining machinery, construction equipment, lifting and transportation machinery, and other hydraulic systems. The series is intended for centralized control of multiple hydraulic actuators within a single valve assembly.
 A modular valve body composed of multiple valve sections is used, allowing different port sizes and flow ratings to be configured as required. Pressure relief valves and check valves can be integrated within the system, enabling the DL series to meet the basic control and protection requirements of a wide range of hydraulic circuits.

title: Modular Flow Control Valve
content:
Modular Flow Control Valve

HCD4 Hydraulic Control Valve

The HCD4 directional-control valve is intended for hydraulic systems used in construction machinery, lifting and transportation equipment, and petroleum and mining machinery. The design enables directional control of hydraulic flow, system pressure regulation, and centralized control of multiple actuators.
 A modular structure is employed, allowing the number of directional sections to be selected as required and combined into parallel, series, or series-parallel circuits, making the valve adaptable to a wide range of hydraulic system configurations.

Features & Advantages

- Multi-stage pressure and load control: Integrated load-holding devices combined with multi-level pressure protection functions enhance overall system safety during operation.

- Adjustable flow: Flow gain characteristics of the directional sections can be customized according to operating conditions, enabling precise matching to different motion response requirements.

- Modular circuit configuration: Support for parallel, series, and series-parallel circuit layouts allows flexible control of multiple actuators within complex hydraulic systems.

- Customizable matching: Based on standard functions, configurations can be adjusted for special operating conditions, facilitating OEM system integration and application-specific optimization.

GCJX Hydraulic Valve

The GCJX series multi spool directional control valves are sectional hydraulic control valves for construction machinery, featuring a cast-in internal flow passage structure. The valve assembly consists of an inlet section with integrated pressure relief valve, standard directional valve sections, and an outlet section, enabling centralized control of multiple hydraulic actuators.
 Both manual control and hydraulic pilot control are supported. Through modular sectional combination, the GCJX series can be configured to meet different system requirements and is suitable for hydraulic systems used in construction machinery, mining machinery, agricultural machinery, coal mining equipment, and metallurgical machinery.

Features & Advantages

- Centralized multi-actuator control: Supports control of up to 10 working functions, making it suitable for multi-circuit hydraulic systems.

- Flexible oil supply modes: Compatible with single-pump or multi-pump supply, allowing easy matching with different system architectures.

- Modular sectional structure: A cast-in internal flow passage sectional design provides high integration and facilitates system configuration and future expansion.

- Series-based specification options: Multiple port sizes and flow ratings are available to meet the selection requirements of different equipment types.

The dump truck custom integrated valve manifold is a custom-designed hydraulic valve assembly developed for belt-type and push-type dump trucks. It is engineered around the specific control requirements of dumping operations, integrating the relevant hydraulic control circuits into a single manifold to simplify overall system architecture. By consolidating functions into an integrated manifold, the system layout is streamlined and external piping is significantly reduced, improving reliability and ease of installation.

- Integrated manifold structure: Reduces external piping and lowers overall system complexity.

- Maintenance-friendly design: Combines cartridge valves and plate-mounted valves, allowing easy inspection, replacement, and servicing.

- Customizable configuration: Can be individually designed according to the complete vehicle hydraulic scheme to meet different dump truck application requirements.

title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifolds for Mobile Machinery Hydraulic Systems | Lite Hydraulic
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifolds

Load-sensing (LS) hydraulic manifolds are widely used in mobile machinery hydraulic systems, where multiple actuators are required to operate simultaneously under varying loads. In such systems, load-sensing hydraulic control valves adjust pump output flow according to the actual pressure demand of the working actuators, rather than supplying constant flow at maximum pressure.
 By regulating pump flow through pressure feedback, load-sensing systems significantly reduce energy consumption in hydraulic systems. The pump only delivers the flow required by the load, avoiding unnecessary throttling losses. This pressure-based flow adjustment also enables smoother actuator motion, reducing friction-related energy loss and minimizing the conversion of excess energy into heat. Lower system heat generation helps slow the thermal aging of hydraulic components, such as seals and valve elements, thereby extending overall system service life and operational stability.
 Load-sensing hydraulic manifolds enable on-demand flow supply and flow sharing during combined actuator operation, balancing operating efficiency and energy saving. Multiple control configurations are supported, including manual control, hydraulic pilot control, electro-hydraulic control and CAN bus integration, making these valves suitable for engineering machinery, mining machinery, agricultural machinery, coal mining equipment and metallurgical machinery.

title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold

MT (Hydraulic Manifold Systems)

The MT series load-sensing hydraulic manifold is a load-feedback hydraulic valve assembly that matches system flow to load demand by detecting actuator load pressure and regulating hydraulic pump output. Within the same valve system, the series supports both pre-compensation load sensing (LS) and load-pressure independent flow distribution (LUDV) control principles, making it suitable for hydraulic systems that require energy efficiency as well as high-performance combined operations.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- LS and LUDV compatibility: Based on load-sensing control, the design accommodates both low-power unloading and flow-sharing requirements during combined operations.

- Flow-sharing capability: During multi-actuator simultaneous operation, load-independent proportional motion can be achieved.

- Flexible control options: Supports manual, hydraulic pilot, electric, and CAN bus control, facilitating integration with different control architectures.

- Strong system tuning capability: Flow-gain characteristics of the directional sections can be customized and are supported by multi-stage pressure protection functions.

title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold

Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG32H

![Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG32H](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/load-sensing-hydraulic-manifold-qfzmg-series-2-2-01m.webp)

The QFZMG32H load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed for high-pressure, high-flow hydraulic systems. It adopts a full load-sensing control concept based on the LUDV principle, achieving proportional flow distribution among multiple actuators through load pressure compensation. This design is well suited for construction machinery hydraulic systems that require continuous combined motions and stable flow distribution, and it supports both hydraulic proportional control and electronic proportional control.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- LUDV flow distribution mechanism: When system flow is insufficient, flow is distributed proportionally among actuators, maintaining continuity of combined operations.

- Proportional control with fine adjustment: Proportional control is supported, and flow can be finely adjusted through spool stroke limiting.

- System stability design: Integrated secondary pressure relief valves and load pressure compensators provide high repeatability and low hysteresis characteristics.

- System-level tuning capability: Supports low-power unloading functions and customizable flow characteristics of the directional sections, enabling effective matching to different operating conditions.

title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold

LT-PSV/PSL (Load-Sensing System)

The LT-PSV/PSL series load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed based on the load-sensing (LS) control principle. By dynamically adjusting system oil supply according to load feedback, the system flow is matched to actuator demand. The series is mainly used for control applications in construction machinery, mining machinery, agricultural machinery, coal mining equipment, and metallurgical machinery, and is suitable for hydraulic systems with requirements for energy efficiency and stable control performance.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- Load-sensing adaptive control: System oil supply is regulated according to actuator load variation, reducing pump load and overall energy consumption.

- Low-power unloading function: Energy loss and system heat generation are reduced under light-load or idle operating conditions.

- Multiple control options: Supports manual control, hydraulic pilot control, electric control, and CAN bus control, facilitating integration with different control systems.

- Series-based configuration: A wide range of port sizes, flow ratings, and section combinations are available to suit hydraulic systems of different scales and capacities.

Drilling Rig Custom Integrated Valve Manifolds

![Hydraulic Control Valve](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/custom-hydraulic-manifold-drilling-equipment-3-02.webp)

Drilling rig integrated valve manifolds serve as the core control unit of drilling rig hydraulic systems, providing centralized control of key operations such as drilling, clamping, and rotation. Based on different drilling operating conditions, the manifold enables combined or independent control of actuators and supports flexible configuration of motion speed and control logic. Through standardized expansion interfaces, the number of directional sections and system functions can be flexibly expanded. Both hydraulic pilot control and electric control options are supported, making the manifold suitable for applications such as coal mine roadway drilling rigs and metal mining core drilling rigs.

Advantages

- Long-term drilling rig application expertise: Since 2009, Lite Hydraulic has focused on the development and manufacture of dedicated integrated valve manifolds for coal mine roadway drilling rigs. Continuous product iteration around drilling processes and hydraulic system characteristics has resulted in multiple generations of mature solutions, which are now widely applied in the domestic drilling equipment market.

title: Joystick Valves for Mobile Machinery Hydraulic Control Systems | Lite Hydraulic
content:
Joystick Valves (Pilot Handle Hydraulic Control Valves)

joystick valves, as well pilot handle hydraulic control valves (Pilot Handles Joystick Controls)

Joystick valves are pilot control components used in mobile machinery hydraulic systems, forming the operator interface between manual input and main directional control valves. Rather than directly handling main system flow, joystick valves generate and manage pilot pressure signals that remotely actuate the spools of main control valves, enabling precise and low-effort operation.
 A typical joystick valve system consists of hydraulic pilot joysticks, which convert handle displacement into proportional pilot pressure, and pilot pressure reducing valves, which regulate and stabilize pilot pressure supply. Together, they ensure consistent control response, smooth motion and reliable multi-function operation under varying system pressures.
 By separating manual operation from the main hydraulic flow, pilot joystick systems reduce operating force, improve controllability and allow flexible layout of operator stations. These systems are widely applied in construction machinery, lifting equipment, mining machinery and other mobile hydraulic applications where precise and coordinated control is required.

The LT-1-2TH6 joystick valves are used in hydraulically piloted multi spool directional control valve systems. Operator handle displacement generates a pilot pressure signal, which remotely actuates the main valve spool, enabling indirect control of high-pressure main circuits via a low-pressure pilot circuit.

The joystick valve supports multi-section combinations and is suitable for various multi-way directional valve systems requiring hydraulic pilot operation.

title: Hydraulic Motors for Mobile and Industrial Hydraulic Systems | Lite Hydraulic
content:
Hydraulic Motors

Hydraulic motors convert hydraulic energy into mechanical rotational motion and are widely used in mobile and industrial hydraulic systems. Depending on system requirements such as speed range, torque output, control flexibility and installation constraints, different motor designs are selected to achieve optimal performance and efficiency.
 In mobile machinery and industrial equipment, hydraulic motors are commonly applied in travel drives, swing mechanisms, winches, conveyors and rotary equipment, where reliable torque output and stable operation are critical. Motor selection is typically based on operating speed, torque demand, pressure level, duty cycle and system layout.

Variable hydraulic Motors

LT-A6V Series (107-160ml/r)

![Variable hydraulic Motors, LT-A6V Series (107-160ml/r)](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/variable-hydraulic-motor-lt-a6v-4-1-01.webp)

![Variable hydraulic Motors, LT-A6V Series (107-160ml/r)](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/variable-hydraulic-motor-lt-a6v-4-1-02.webp)

The LT-A6V variable hydraulic motors are suitable for both open-circuit and closed-circuit hydraulic transmission systems. With a wide displacement adjustment range, the motor can flexibly switch between high-speed and high-torque operating conditions. Motor output speed is proportional to input flow and inversely proportional to displacement, while output torque increases with system pressure differential and displacement. This makes the LT-A6V series well suited for hydraulic drive systems requiring continuous speed control and adaptability to multiple operating conditions.

content:
Hydraulic Pumps

Hydraulic pumps are the primary power source in hydraulic systems, responsible for converting mechanical energy into hydraulic energy in the form of flow and pressure. In mobile machinery and industrial equipment, hydraulic pumps not only determine system power capacity, but also directly influence efficiency, controllability and system integration.

About LITE

About LITE

![图片](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/imgs/about-lite.webp)

Jinzhou Lite Hydraulic Technology Co., Ltd. (Lite Hydraulic) is a hydraulic products manufacturer with over 30 years of industry experience. The company focus on the development and manufacturing of hydraulic components including hydraulic valve, motors and pumps for a variety of industries such as construction, mining, agricultural, marine, metallurgy and environmental sanitation.

Lite Hydraulic operates its own production facility covering approximately 40,000 square meters. title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold

Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG12H

![Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG12H](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/load-sensing-hydraulic-manifold-qfzmg-series-2-2-36m.webp)

The QFZMG12H load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed based on the pre-compensation load-sensing (LS) principle. It delivers the required flow according to actuator demand while maintaining actuator speed independent of load variation. This design is suitable for construction machinery hydraulic systems that require high inching performance and precise control, and it supports multiple control methods to meet different system configuration requirements.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- Pre-compensation load-sensing control: Stable flow supply is achieved through load feedback, making the valve suitable for fine and precise motion control.

- Excellent inching performance: Enhances controllability and accuracy under low-speed and small-stroke operating conditions.

- Energy-efficient design: Under load-sensing control, system energy loss and power consumption are effectively reduced.

title: Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold
content:
Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold

Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG14H

![Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG14H](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/load-sensing-hydraulic-manifold-qfzmg-series-2-2-29m.webp)

The QFZMG14H load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed based on the LUDV principle, enabling load-pressure-independent flow distribution between individual valve sections. This makes it suitable for hydraulic systems that require proportional consistency during combined operations.
 A compact sectional structure is adopted, allowing different valve assemblies to be formed as required. Compatibility with both fixed displacement pumps and variable displacement pumps makes the valve suitable for a wide range of hydraulic control applications.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- LUDV flow distribution mechanism: Flow distribution of each valve section is independent of load pressure, ensuring stable proportional motion during combined operations.

- Wide pump compatibility: Suitable for use with both fixed displacement pump systems and variable displacement pump systems.

- Flexible system protection and control: Supports multiple control methods and is equipped with multi-stage pressure protection functions.

- Customizable operating-condition matching: Configuration adjustments can be made on the basis of the standard structure to meet the requirements of different equipment and applications.

This design is well suited for construction machinery hydraulic systems that require high control accuracy and excellent inching performance, and it supports multiple control methods to meet different system configurations.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- Pre-compensation load-sensing control: Stable flow control is achieved through load feedback, making the valve suitable for precision motion applications.

- Excellent inching performance: Designed for operating conditions that demand smooth motion and high controllability.

- Multiple control options: Supports manual control, hydraulic pilot control, electric control, and CAN bus control, facilitating flexible system integration.

- Customizable operating-condition matching: Configuration and parameter tuning can be carried out on the basis of standard functions to adapt to different application requirements.

Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG28H

![Load-Sensing Hydraulic Manifold, QFZMG28H](https://hydraulicproducts-en.com/products/load-sensing-hydraulic-manifold-qfzmg-series-2-2-10m.webp)

The QFZMG28H load-sensing hydraulic manifold is designed based on the LUDV principle and is suitable for closed-center variable displacement pump systems. Proportional flow distribution among multiple actuators is achieved through load pressure compensation. Targeted at high-pressure, high-flow applications, the valve is mainly used in construction machinery such as rotary drilling rigs and crawler cranes, where continuity of combined motions is critical.

Features & Advantages
 Selection Guidelines

 Features & Advantages

- LUDV flow distribution mechanism: When system flow is insufficient, flow is distributed proportionally among actuators, maintaining continuous combined operation.

- Multi-actuator control capability: Supports control of up to 8 actuators, suitable for complex hydraulic system configurations.

- System stability design: Integrates load-holding valves, secondary pressure relief valves, and load pressure compensators, delivering high repeatability and low hysteresis.

- Proportional control and tuning capability: Utilizes hydraulic pilot proportional control and supports flow adjustment via spool stroke limiting.
